content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Water Taps". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/954/problem/E
content.inputFormat: The input consists of multiple test cases. Each test case begins with a line containing two integers n and m, where n is the number of houses and m is the number of taps. Then, there are n lines, each containing the position of a house. After that, there are m lines, each containing the position of a tap.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the minimum cost to connect all houses to taps.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a greedy algorithm to assign each house to the closest tap.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N + M log M)

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, M = map(int, input().split())
    houses = [int(input()) for _ in range(N)]
    taps = [int(input()) for _ in range(M)]
    houses.sort()
    taps.sort()
    cost = sum(abs(houses[i] - taps[i % M]) for i in range(N))
    print(cost)
